title: Mighty Moist Dutch Egg Omelette
description: A winey, fluffy, and Whole30-friendly Dutch dish featuring dill and lamination-puff, perfect for a hangover cure.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, dill, salt, black pepper, and garlic powder.
Add the dry white wine to the egg mixture and whisk until well combined.
Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Pour the egg mixture into the skillet and cook until the edges start to set.
Place the skillet in the oven and bake for 10 minutes, or until the eggs are almost set.
Remove the skillet from the oven and place the puff pastry on top of the eggs.
Return the skillet to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Slice the omelette into wedges and serve hot.
